response.justification: The candidate is a strong full-stack/web software engineer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and embedded/mobile work, but the role requires deep SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance architecture, GPU/SIMD/MIMD expertise, and hardware-software co-design for deep learning workloads. The resume shows essentially no overlap with chip-level performance modeling, deep learning training/inference optimization, or accelerator architecture. Educational background is a BS in CS without the specialized hardware/architecture focus, and while years of experience meet the minimum threshold, the technical domain is a fundamental mismatch for a Principal-level hardware architecture role.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) as a software engineer across many domains
Some low-level/systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and DLang high-performance work at Sociomantic
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testability, public talks and workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator architecture experience
No demonstrated work on deep neural network training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD systems
Career is web/full-stack focused; lacks the specialized performance-architecture depth expected at Principal level

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC architecture expertise
No deep learning workload or GPU performance analysis background
Solid general software engineering breadth and seniority
